sections.paragraph:
οἰκοδομία, ας, ἡ (οἰκοδομέω; since Thu. 1, 93, 1; 2, 65, 2; Pla.; Polyb. 10, 22, 7; Plut., Pomp. 654 [66, 1]; Lucian, Hist. Conscr. 4; Jos., Ant. 11, 7; 118; SIG 144, 32 [IV b.c.]; 204, 26f al.; OGI 483, 104; 107; PHal 1, 181 [III b.c.]; PSI 500, 3; 4.—Lob., Phryn. p. 487) building, both as process and result, in our lit. only once as v.l., and in a fig. sense edification ἐκζητήσεις παρέχουσιν μᾶλλον ἢ οἰκοδομίαν θεοῦ 1 Ti 1:4 v.l. (for οἰκονομίαν; D, Irenaeus et al. have οἰκοδομήν).—M-M.

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

PHal PHal = Dikaiomata: in a papyrus published by the Graeca Halensis—List 4

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Lob. Lob. = CLobeck, s. Phryn.—List 5

Phryn Phryn , the Atticist, signifying his Eclogae, II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 697
